creature.def: 生物、動物、創造物人物 (單數) (物)
creature.col: strange creature, creature of habit (習慣的奴隸), living creature
creature.ex: I am simple creature of habit and joy.

creaturely.def: 生物的、肉體的的人的人物
creaturely.ex: He enjoys all the creaturely comforts.

creche/crèche.def: 托兒所、幼兒園、育嬰室、馬槽(聖誕裝飾)人物 (單數) (幼)
creche/crèche.ex: She leaves her baby at the local creche.

credence.def: 相信、信任、憑據的人物 (單數) (信)
credence.col: give credence to, letter of credence
credence.ex: Don't give any credence to his rumors.

credential.def: 憑據的人物、外交使節的身分的人的人人物 (單數)
credential.ex: He has the best academic credentials.

credentials.def: 證書、資格、國書人物 (複數使用) (證)

credibility.def: 可靠性、可信度的人物 (單數) (信)
credibility.ex: The news report lacks absolute credibility.

credible.ex: He is a very highly credible witness.

credibly.ex: The event was reported credibly today.

credit.def: 信用、信譽、貸款、學分、榮譽、賒購的人的人人物 (單數) (信)
credit.col: on credit, credit card, give credit to, tax credit
credit.ex: You must give credit to his hard work.

creditable.ex: He gave a very creditable performance.

creditably.ex: He behaved creditably in the crisis.

creditor.def: 債權人、債主人物 (單數) (債)
creditor.ex: The main creditor asked for the money.

creditors.def: 債權人人物 (複數使用) (債)
creditors.ex: The meeting of creditors starts today.

creditworthiness.def: 信譽、信用可靠的人物 (單數) (信)

creditworthy.def: 有信用的、值得信賴的人的人物 (信)
creditworthy.ex: He is a very creditworthy borrower.

credo.ex: Simple truth is her political credo.

credulity.def: 輕信、易受騙的人物 (單數) (信)
credulity.ex: His tale managed to tax my credulity.

credulous.ex: She has a very kind and credulous nature.

creed.def: 信條、教義、信念人物 (單數) (信)
creed.ex: People of every creed live here now.

creek.def: 小溪、小河、小灣人物 (單數) (溪)
creek.col: up the creek (在困境中), stony creek
creek.ex: We caught small fish in the stony creek.

creel.ex: He put the trout in a willow creel.

creep.def: 爬行、悄悄移動、(口)毛骨悚然、賂媚的人的人人物 (單數) (爬)
creep.col: give the creeps, creep up on, learn to creep
creep.ex: Old houses always give me the creeps.

creeper.def: 爬行物、蔓藤、爬蟲類人物 (單數) (爬)
creeper.ex: A flowering creeper grew on the wall.

creepy.ex: I have a very creepy feeling about this.

cremate.ex: The family decided to cremate the body.

cremation.ex: The cremation took place this morning.

crematorium.ex: The local crematorium is on the hill.

crenate.def: 有圓齒的、波狀的人的人物 (波)
crenate.ex: The fern has a beautiful crenate leaf.

crenellated.def: 有垛口的的人的人 (或物) (垛)
crenellated.ex: The castle has a high crenellated wall.

crenellation.ex: The stone crenellation was very old.

creole.def: 克里奧爾人、混合語人物 (單數) (語)
creole.ex: She loves spicy local creole cooking.

creosote.def: 雜酚油、防腐劑的人的人人物 (單數) (油)
creosote.ex: The fence smelled strongly of creosote.

crepe/crape.def: 縐紗、法式薄餅的人的人人物 (單數) (餅/紗)
crepe/crape.ex: She made a sweet banana crepe today.

crepitate.ex: The dry twigs began to crepitate now.

crepitation.ex: There was a soft crepitation in lung.

crepuscular.def: 黃昏的、微光的人的人物 (昏)
crepuscular.ex: He loves the soft crepuscular light.

crescendo.def: (樂)漸強、頂峰的人的人人物 (單數) (強)
crescendo.ex: The noise rose to a loud crescendo.

crescent.def: 新月、新月形、增長的的人的人人物 (單數) (月)
crescent.ex: The crescent moon shone in the sky.

cress.ex: He added fresh water cress to salad.

crest.def: 冠、頂、浪頭、家徽的人物、達到頂峰的人的人人物 (單數) (頂)
crest.col: crest of wave, family crest, mountain crest
crest.ex: The family crest was on the silver.

crestfallen.ex: He looked crestfallen after his loss.

cretaceous.def: 白堊的、白堊紀的人的人物 (單數) (堊)

crevasse.def: 冰河裂縫、深淵人物 (單數) (裂)
crevasse.ex: The climber fell into a deep crevasse.

crevice.ex: A lizard hid in the small rock crevice.

crew.def: 全體人員、船員、一隊的人的人人物 (單數) (隊)
crew.col: flight crew, film crew, television crew
crew.ex: The flight crew prepared for takeoff.

crewcut.ex: He has a very short and neat crewcut.

crewel.def: 刺繡用毛線人物 (單數) (線)
crewel.ex: She is doing some crewel embroidery.

crib.def: 幼兒床、抄襲、翻譯的人的人人物 (單數) (床/抄)
crib.ex: He used a crib sheet during the test.

cribbage.def: (牌)克里比奇牌戲人物 (單數) (牌)
cribbage.ex: They spent the night playing cribbage.

crick.ex: He woke up with a bad crick in neck.

cricket.def: 蟋蟀、板球、正當行為的人物 (單數) (蟋/球)
cricket.col: not cricket (不公平), play cricket, chirping cricket
cricket.ex: The chirping cricket kept me awake.

cricketer.def: 板球運動員人物 (單數) (球)
cricketer.ex: He is a famed cricketer for our town.

crier.def: 呼喊者、報信者人物 (單數) (報)
crier.ex: The town crier shouted the latest news.

crime.def: 犯罪、罪行、極其遺憾的事人物 (單數) (罪)
crime.col: organize crime, commit a crime, war crime
crime.ex: He was accused of a very serious crime.

criminal.def: 犯罪的、刑事的人、罪犯的人物 (單數) (罪)
criminal.col: criminal record, criminal law, hardened criminal
criminal.ex: The police caught a hardened criminal.

criminality.def: 犯罪、犯罪性、罪行人物 (單數) (罪)
criminality.ex: The city has a high level of criminality.

criminalize.def: 宣告為非法、使成罪的人的人
criminalize.ex: The goal is to criminalize the drug use.

criminally.ex: He was found criminally liable for it.

criminate.def: 控訴的人的人、使負罪的人的人
criminate.ex: They tried to criminate him for theft.

crimination.def: 控訴、負罪的人的人物 (單數) (控)
crimination.ex: He avoided any form of self-crimination.

criminative.ex: The police had some criminative data.

criminologist.ex: An expert criminologist gave a talk.

criminology.ex: He decided to study modern criminology.

crimson.def: 深紅色的、(羞)臉紅、深紅色的人物 (單數) (紅)
crimson.ex: Her face turned crimson with shame.

cringe.def: 畏縮、賂媚、肉麻的人的人人物 (單數) (縮)
cringe.col: cringe with fear, total cringe (極其尷尬)
cringe.ex: I began to cringe at his terrible jokes.

crinkle.def: 起皺、捲曲的人物、使起皺的人的人人物 (單數) (皺)
crinkle.ex: The thin paper began to crinkle now.

crinkled/crinkly.def: 起皺的、多波的人的人 (或物) (皺)
crinkled/crinkly.ex: She had a lot of gray and crinkly hair.

crinoline.def: 裙襯、馬尾襯、克里諾林裙人物 (單數) (裙)
crinoline.ex: She wore a massive hooped crinoline.

cripple.def: 跛子、殘廢的人的人物、使殘廢的人的人人物 (單數) (殘)
cripple.ex: The war managed to cripple the economy.

crippled.def: 殘障的、傷殘了的人的人 (或物) (殘)
crippled.ex: The crippled soldier returned to home.

crippling.def: 嚴重的、致殘的人 (或物) (重)
crippling.ex: They suffered from a crippling local debt.

crisis.def: 危機、緊要關頭、(醫)轉折點的人物 (單數) (危)
crisis.col: financial crisis, energy crisis, in crisis
crisis.ex: The whole world is in a financial crisis.

crisp.def: 脆的、鮮脆的、炸薯片、簡潔地的人的人人物 (單數) (脆)
crisp.col: crisp air, potato crisp, crisp reply
crisp.ex: He gave a very short and crisp reply.

crisply.ex: The officer spoke crisply and fast.

crispy.ex: I love eating warm and crispy chicken.
